summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a high‑energy Europe ’72 show highlighted by a blazing “Greatest Story Ever Told” opener and stellar renditions of Tennessee Jed, Brown Eyed Women, and Looks Like Rain.
review: The recording is praised as a clean, well‑balanced soundboard source that finally brings this historic April 1972 concert to fans. The performance itself is described as “cooking,” with an energetic opening of “Greatest Story Ever Told” and standout versions of new‑era staples like Tennessee Jed, Brown Eyed Women, and the pedal‑steel‑enhanced “Looks Like Rain.” Bob Weir’s second‑guitar work receives special commendation, and Jerry’s on‑stage banter adds to the excitement.

ai_rating.rationale: Single detailed 5‑star review emphasizes both superb musical performance and a clean soundboard recording, meriting a high rating despite limited sample size.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No technical complaints; reviewers note the release finally provides a clear, balanced mix of this historic show.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Energetic opening leads into a seamless set of newer repertoire; the band maintains high intensity throughout, exemplifying the classic Europe ’72 cooking vibe.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His on‑stage comment “We’re makin’ a record man.” is highlighted, underscoring his charismatic presence.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s (Bob Weir) second‑guitar parts are praised as going far beyond mere rhythm.
